_metadata.description: Field reference cards for all custom modes — structured for iPhone app consumption
_metadata.card_format: Each card has a FRONT (physical setup + field adjustments) and BACK (saved software settings + rationale + hints + educational text)

modes.name: Street / General
modes.description: Walk-around photography. Markets, parks, towns, beaches. The 'pick up and shoot' mode.

field_adjustments.text: f/2.8-4 → blurs background, isolates subject
f/8-11 → everything sharp (street scenes, architecture)
Bright sun: close down (f/8+). Shade: open up (f/2.8-4)
Camera sets shutter speed automatically — just shoot

software_settings.rationale: You pick DoF; camera handles speed

hints: Tap touchscreen to move AF point to your subject
If SS drops below 1/60 in the viewfinder, brace camera or open aperture
For quick candids, leave at f/5.6 — good all-around sharpness
back.why: Aperture Priority: aperture is the creative decision (how much blur?) while shutter speed is just technical (fast enough to avoid shake). Control the one thing that matters, let the camera handle the rest. In good light the camera will never pick a bad shutter speed.

modes.name: Portrait / People
modes.description: People, environmental portraits, group photos. Face/eye detection does the hard work.

field_adjustments.text: f/1.7-2.8 → creamy blur for individual portraits
f/5.6-8 → group photos (everyone in focus)
Camera detects faces/eyes automatically — trust the AF
Move your feet, don't zoom — step back for full body, closer for headshot

hints: Open shade gives the most flattering light on faces
Check eyes are sharp after each shot — re-shoot if AF missed
Avoid direct overhead sun — causes harsh shadows under eyes/nose
back.why: Face/eye detection locks onto eyes with remarkable accuracy. Wide apertures (f/1.7-2.8) separate the person from the background — the classic portrait look. For groups, close down to f/5.6-8 so everyone is in the focal plane.

modes.name: Landscape
modes.description: Scenery, vistas, nature. Maximum sharpness across the entire frame.

physical_setup.note: then rear dial to select C3-1

field_adjustments.note: controls depth of field across scene

field_adjustments.text: f/8-11 → SWEET SPOT for MFT — sharpest landscapes
Never go past f/16 — diffraction softens the image
Focus 1/3 into the scene, NOT on the horizon
Golden hour (sunrise/sunset) = best landscape light

software_settings.rationale: Stays at ISO 200 in good light
Tap screen to pick focus point
No subject to detect in scenery
Or set Daylight for golden hour

hints: If shutter drops below 1/60, use tripod or brace firmly
Photo Style Scenery boosts colors — great for nature
Use 2s timer to avoid camera shake on tripod at slow speeds
back.why: Landscapes need front-to-back sharpness. On MFT sensors, f/8-11 gives deep DoF without diffraction softening (which starts past f/16). Focus 1/3 into the scene to maximize DoF coverage — not on the horizon.

modes.name: Macro + Flash
modes.description: Handheld close-ups: insects, frogs, snakes, lizards. Off-camera flash is the light source.

physical_setup.note: then rear dial to select C3-2
or swap: 60mm f/2.8 for small insects

field_adjustments.text: DO NOT TOUCH camera dials — all exposure settings are FIXED
Too bright → lower X3 power. Too dark → raise X3 power
TTL works 0.5x-1x on MkI. Above 1x: switch X3 to M 1/128
Focus: rock your body in/out. Focus ring for coarse adjust

software_settings.rationale: Flash is the light; ambient irrelevant
AF hunts at macro — rock to focus

hints: 90mm = safer working distance (frogs, snakes). 60mm = more magnification (insects)
Flash fires at ~1/10000s — freezes all motion regardless of shutter speed
At 1:1 magnification, DoF at f/11 is about 1mm. Precision matters!
back.why: In flash macro, the flash IS the light system. Ambient is irrelevant — the flash fires at ~1/10000s, freezing motion and providing all illumination. Everything is fixed: f/11 for DoF, 1/200 for sync, ISO 200 for clean files. The only variable is flash power, adjusted on the X3 trigger.

modes.name: Wildlife / Action
modes.description: Birds, animals in motion. Continuous tracking, high-speed burst.

physical_setup.note: then rear dial to select C3-3

field_adjustments.note: open wider in shade for lower ISO

field_adjustments.text: f/2.8 in shade (max light) → f/4-5.6 in bright sun
1/2000 baseline. Raise to 1/4000+ for fast birds
Half-press and HOLD to track — AF locks on animal automatically
Zoom out slightly — crop later. In frame > missed entirely
Accept ISO 3200-6400. Noisy sharp > clean blurry

hints: M + Auto ISO = 'wildlife triangle': lock aperture + speed, ISO floats
Zone AF gives generous tracking area — no need for surgical framing
Keep both eyes open to track subject outside the viewfinder
back.why: Manual + Auto ISO guarantees your two critical variables: f/2.8 for max light gathering, 1/2000s for freezing motion. The camera just adjusts ISO. AFC + Animal Detection + Zone AF: camera recognizes animals, locks on, and tracks through the frame.

modes.name: Street / General
modes.description: Walk-around photography. Markets, parks, towns, beaches. Point and shoot.

field_adjustments.text: f/2.8-4 → blurs background, isolates subject
f/8-11 → everything sharp (street, architecture)
Bright sun: close down (f/8+). Shade: open up (f/2.8-4)
Camera sets shutter speed automatically — just shoot

software_settings.rationale: You pick DoF; camera handles speed

hints: Tap touchscreen to move AF point to your subject
Phase-detect AF on MkII is faster — more 'point and shoot' reliable
If SS drops below 1/60, brace camera or open aperture wider
back.why: Aperture Priority: control the one creative decision (blur vs. sharpness), let the camera handle speed and ISO. The G9II's phase-detect AF is faster and more accurate than the MkI, making this mode extremely reliable.

modes.name: Portrait / People
modes.description: People, environmental portraits, group photos. Face/eye detection.

field_adjustments.text: f/1.7-2.8 → creamy blur for individual portraits
f/5.6-8 → group photos (everyone in focus)
Camera detects faces/eyes automatically — trust the AF
Move your feet, don't zoom — step closer or farther

hints: Open shade = most flattering portrait light
Check eyes are sharp after each shot — re-shoot if AF missed
Phase-detect AF on MkII locks eyes even at f/1.7 razor-thin DoF
back.why: The G9II's phase-detect AF with Human detection locks onto eyes with sub-second precision, even at wide apertures. Wide aperture separates person from background. For groups, close to f/5.6-8 so everyone is in the focal plane.

modes.name: Landscape
modes.description: Scenery, vistas, nature. Maximum sharpness, enhanced colors.

physical_setup.note: then rear dial to select C3-1

field_adjustments.text: f/8-11 → SWEET SPOT — sharpest landscapes on MFT
Never go past f/16 — diffraction softens the image
Focus 1/3 into the scene, NOT on the horizon
Golden hour (sunrise/sunset) = best landscape light

software_settings.rationale: Stays at ISO 200 in good light
Or set Daylight for golden hour

hints: If shutter drops below 1/60, use tripod or brace firmly
Scenery photo style boosts greens and blues for nature
25MP sensor captures excellent detail at f/8-11
back.why: On MFT, f/8-11 gives deep DoF without diffraction (starts at f/16). Focus 1/3 into the scene for maximum DoF coverage. AF Detection OFF because you're focusing on a distance, not a subject. Scenery style enhances landscape colors.

modes.name: Macro + Flash
modes.description: Handheld close-ups: insects, frogs, small creatures. Off-camera flash.

physical_setup.note: then rear dial to select C3-2

field_adjustments.text: DO NOT TOUCH camera dials — all exposure settings are FIXED
TTL OVEREXPOSES on MkII — always use X3 in Manual mode
Too bright → lower power. Too dark → raise power
Focus: rock your body in/out. Focus ring for coarse adjust

software_settings.rationale: Rock to focus at macro distance
MkII EVF goes black with flash

hints: TTL overexposes because metering sees dark background and overcompensates
Constant Preview simulates ambient in M mode — near-black with flash as main light
This issue does NOT exist on the MkI — its preview doesn't fully simulate M mode
back.why: MkII CRITICAL: TTL overexposes at all macro distances. Use Manual 1/128 on X3. Constant Preview MUST be OFF — MkII simulates ambient exposure in EVF, which is near-zero when flash is the main light. Flash fires at ~1/10000s, freezing all motion.

modes.name: Wildlife / Action
modes.description: Birds, animals in motion. Continuous tracking, high-speed burst.

physical_setup.note: then rear dial to select C3-3

field_adjustments.note: open wider in shade for less noise

field_adjustments.text: f/6.3 at 400mm (max). Open wider at shorter focal lengths
1/2000 baseline. Raise to 1/4000+ for fast birds
Half-press and HOLD to track — AF locks on animal
Zoom out slightly — crop later. In frame > missed
Accept ISO 6400-12800. Noisy sharp > clean blurry

hints: Phase-detect AF on MkII recognizes birds/animals by shape, locks on eyes
Zone AF gives generous area — no surgical framing needed in the heat of action
M + Auto ISO = 'wildlife triangle': lock aperture + speed, ISO compensates
back.why: f/6.3 matches the Leica 100-400's max at 400mm. M + Auto ISO: lock both critical variables (aperture for light, speed for freezing), let ISO float. Phase-detect AF with Animal Detection tracks through the frame automatically.

modes.name: Tripod Macro Stack
modes.description: Focus bracketing on tripod. Multiple shots merged in post for full sharpness.

physical_setup.note: then rear dial to select C3-4

field_adjustments.text: Focus on the NEAREST point of the subject before starting
Don't touch camera after pressing shutter — 2s delay
~2 fps mechanical shutter — 5 frames takes ~3 sec (hardware limit)
Stack in software after — camera captures the raw frames

software_settings.rationale: All fixed; flash is the light

hints: f/8 (not f/11): sharper individual frames — stacking provides the depth
IS OFF on tripod: stabilizer micro-corrections shift frames out of alignment
NOT for Costa Rica trip — tripod macro is a studio/controlled technique
back.why: Focus stacking: multiple shots at different focus distances, merged for full sharpness. At 1:1, f/8 gives ~0.5mm DoF per frame. f/8 over f/11 for sharper frames — stacking adds depth. IS OFF prevents micro-shifts.

modes.name: Birds + Crop Zoom
modes.description: Extra reach for distant birds. Digital 2x crop = 800mm equivalent at 400mm.

physical_setup.note: then rear dial to select C3-5

field_adjustments.text: Use zoom lever to engage Crop Zoom (up to 2.0x)
400mm + 2.0x crop = 800mm MFT equivalent — extreme reach
JPEG ONLY — no RAW available with Crop Zoom
Same shooting technique as C3-3: half-press, track, burst

hints: Identical to C3-3 except for Crop Zoom ON and JPEG-only output
Use C3-3 when you have enough reach; switch to C3-5 when you don't
Resolution is reduced (digital crop) — only use when needed
back.why: Crop Zoom trades resolution for reach: camera uses center of sensor, doubling focal length. 400mm becomes 800mm equivalent. Trade-off: JPEG only, smaller file. Otherwise identical to C3-3.

modes.name: Lightning
modes.description: Live View Composite on tripod. Each bolt is added to the image. Press Q to stop.

physical_setup.note: then rear dial to select C3-6

field_adjustments.text: Frame the scene. Pre-focus on landscape/horizon with MF
Press shutter to START — camera takes continuous 4s frames
Watch the screen: each bolt is ADDED (only new bright pixels)
Press Q button to STOP when you have enough bolts
If sky gets too bright, stop sooner — it builds up over time
DO NOT touch camera during session

hints: LVC only adds pixels BRIGHTER than previous frame — sky stays dark, bolts accumulate
Daylight WB is fixed (not AWB) to prevent color shifts between frames
16:9 cinematic aspect — suited to wide storm panoramas
back.why: Live View Composite: repeated 4s exposures, only adding brighter pixels. Sky stays dark while each bolt accumulates. One image with multiple bolts. Press Q to end.

modes.name: Indoor / Low Light
modes.description: Museums, churches, quiet venues. Completely silent — no flash, no shutter sound.

physical_setup.note: then rear dial to select C3-7

field_adjustments.note: keep f/2.8 unless you need DoF

field_adjustments.text: Camera is COMPLETELY SILENT — safe for museums and quiet spaces
Brace against wall, pillar, or doorframe in very low light
If photos are blurry: accept higher ISO rather than slower shutter

hints: Silent Mode = truly invisible. Zero noise. Essential for museums/churches
If subjects are static (paintings), can drop to 1/30 with good bracing
Electronic shutter may band under flickering lights — rare but possible
back.why: Silent Mode: zero mechanical noise, essential where shutter clicks are disruptive. f/2.8 wide open gathers max light. Auto ISO floats to 12800. Key field adjustment: shutter speed — static subjects allow 1/30-1/60.

modes.name: Birds in Flight (BIF)
modes.description: Fast-moving birds. 1/4000s freeze, tracking AF, panning stabilization.

physical_setup.note: then rear dial to select C3-8

field_adjustments.text: IS Mode 2 = PANNING — only vertical axis stabilized
1/4000 baseline. Raise to 1/6000+ for hummingbirds
Pre-focus on area where bird will fly. Track with smooth body motion
Overcast sky is easier — harsh sun creates extreme contrast

hints: Tracking AF (not Zone) is more aggressive — follows across entire frame
IS Mode 2 allows smooth horizontal panning; Mode 1 would fight the pan
Difference from C3-3: faster shutter, Tracking (not Zone), IS Mode 2 for panning
back.why: BIF is the most demanding scenario. 1/4000s freezes wing beats. Tracking AF follows across frame. IS Mode 2: vertical-only stabilization allows smooth panning without fighting horizontal tracking.

modes.name: Video — Travel
modes.description: Documentary, travel clips, vlogs. Fully automatic exposure — just press record.

physical_setup.note: then rear dial to select C3-9

field_adjustments.text: P mode — camera handles EVERYTHING automatically
Just press the red record button and go
Keep movements slow and smooth — jerky pans look amateur
Avoid zooming while recording — almost never looks good
Tap screen to shift AF to a different person
Record 10-15 second clips. Shorter is better for editing

hints: P mode = zero decisions while recording. Camera adjusts everything
4K 30p: high res for cropping, 30fps is natural for documentary
E-Stab STANDARD adds stabilization without the crop of HIGH mode
back.why: Program mode for video: zero decisions during recording. 4K 30p balances resolution with natural documentary look. E-Stab STANDARD adds digital stabilization without excessive crop.

modes.name: Video — Wildlife
modes.description: Animals in motion. Manual exposure, tracking AF, strong stabilization.

physical_setup.note: then rear dial to select C3-10

field_adjustments.text: Tracking AF follows the animal — tap screen to select target
E-Stab HIGH crops slightly but gives much smoother footage
Start recording BEFORE the action — you can trim, can't rewind
Keep steady — at 400mm every shake is magnified

hints: Manual exposure prevents brightness jumps when panning sky → forest
1/500s video balance: freezes per-frame motion without stuttery look
E-Stab HIGH: aggressive digital stab, worth the crop for handheld telephoto
back.why: Manual for wildlife video: prevents exposure jumps across brightness zones. 1/500s balances motion freeze without stuttery look. E-Stab HIGH: slight crop, essential for handheld telephoto.
